description: A psychology instructor has collected Corsi Block-Tapping Task data from 21 participants (CB01-CB20 and sub-X99) at ~/pebl/data/corsi_block_data.csv. The CSV includes columns for participant_id, direction (forward or backward), sequence_length (2-9), attempt (1 or 2), correct (1=correct, 0=error), and response_time_ms. Compute the spatial working memory span and product score for each valid participant in both directions. Span is the longest sequence length at which the participant scored at least one correct out of two attempts; failing both attempts at a length ends the span. Product Score is the Span multiplied by the total number of correct trials for that direction. Exclude participant sub-X99 from group calculations due to data contamination resulting in physiologically impossible performance. Produce a JSON report at ~/pebl/analysis/corsi_span_report.json containing a 'participants' list with 'id', 'forward_span', 'backward_span', 'forward_product_score', 'backward_product_score', 'forward_mean_rt_ms', and 'backward_mean_rt_ms' (or 'excluded': true and a 'reason' for sub-X99). The report must also include group means across all valid participants: 'group_forward_span_mean', 'group_backward_span_mean', 'group_forward_product_mean', and 'group_backward_product_mean'.

metadata.data_source: Derived from Kessels et al. (2000) applied to PEBL Battery
metadata.reference: Kessels, R.P.C., et al. (2000). Applied Neuropsychology, 7(4), 252-258.
